top_signals.title: Korea Watchdog Chief Regrets Single-Stock Leveraged ETF Launch
top_signals.summary: South Korea’s top financial regulator said he regrets not blocking the launch of leveraged exchange-traded funds tracking single stocks, warning their negative side effects have grown significantly.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Iranian Crude Oil Exports Surge Via Hormuz as More Ships Transit - Bloomberg.com

top_signals.title: Permian natural gas production increased faster than crude oil
top_signals.summary: The Permian region's marketed natural gas production grew from 17.2 billion cubic feet per day (Bcf/d) in 2021 to 27.6 Bcf/d in 2025, a 60% increase, according to data from our latest Short Term Energy Outlook. Over the same period, crude oil production grew by 39%, going from 4.7 million barrels per day (b/d) to 6.6 million b/d. The higher growth in natural gas production is the result of increasing gas-oil ratios (GOR).
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Yen continues to test fresh lows as Nikkei 225 hits new highs
top_signals.summary: Verbal intervention in the currency market has been more tempered than in the past.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: David Lloyd gyms limbers up for £4bn London float
top_signals.summary: The owner of David Lloyd gyms is considering a £4bn London listing in what would be a major boost for the capital’s stock exchange. TDR Capital, which also owns Asda, is eyeing up the initial public offering (IPO) despite a dearth of high-profile listings in recent years, City AM understands. The private equity firm snapped [...]
